exercises.title: The Wins Journal Challenge
exercises.description: Start a daily practice of writing down 3 wins from each day. They can be small (made someone laugh, finished a task) or big (landed a client, received praise). For each win, write why it matters and how it shows your capability.
hints: Keep the journal by your bed so you do it before sleep
Don't dismiss small wins — they add up
Include wins from all areas of life, not just work

solution.code:
Wins Journal - Week 1:

Day 1:
1. Spoke up in meeting (why: I usually stay quiet)
2. Finished report ahead of deadline (why: shows I can manage time)
3. Helped colleague solve problem (why: my knowledge is valuable)

Day 2:
1. Had difficult conversation with manager (why: I handled it maturely)
2. Ran 3 miles without stopping (why: I'm building endurance)
3. Received positive client feedback (why: my work matters)

Day 3:
1. Said 'no' to extra work (why: I'm setting boundaries)
2. Learned new tool in 30 min (why: I can learn quickly)
3. Made new friend at event (why: I can connect with people)

Key insight: After 7 days, I noticed I was more willing to take risks. The journal made my capabilities tangible.

exercises.title: The Comfort Zone Expansion Plan
exercises.description: Identify 5 things outside your comfort zone that would help you grow. Create a 5-week plan, tackling one each week. Start with the easiest and work up to the hardest. After each, write down what you learned and how it felt.
hints: Choose things that are uncomfortable but not terrifying

solution.code:
Comfort Zone Expansion Plan:

Week 1 (Easy): Introduce myself to a stranger at a coffee shop
Result: Felt awkward but survived. Realized most people are friendly.

Week 2 (Medium): Speak up first in a team meeting
Result: My idea was actually good! People listened and built on it.

Week 3 (Medium): Give a 5-minute presentation to my team
Result: Heart racing, but I prepared well. Received positive feedback.

Week 4 (Hard): Negotiate my next project's deadline
Result: Asked for 2 extra weeks, got 1. Better than nothing!

Week 5 (Hardest): Lead a meeting with senior leadership
Result: Terrifying but transformative. Proved to myself I could do it.

Key insight: Each week, the previous challenge felt easier. The growth was exponential, not linear.

exercises.title: The Imposter Syndrome Reframe
exercises.description: When you feel like an imposter, complete this worksheet: (1) Write the imposter thought verbatim. (2) List evidence that contradicts it. (3) Write a reframe that acknowledges the feeling but focuses on growth. (4) Identify one action you can take today to build evidence of competence.
hints: Be specific with evidence — vague statements don't help
The reframe should be realistic, not positive toxic positivity
Focus on what you can control, not what you can't

solution.code:
Imposter Syndrome Reframe Worksheet:

Imposter thought: 'I don't deserve this promotion. Everyone will find out I'm not qualified.'

Evidence contradicting:
1. I've exceeded my targets for 3 consecutive quarters
2. My manager specifically recommended me for the role
3. I've received positive feedback from 5 different colleagues
4. I have 7 years of experience in this field
5. I completed the leadership training program with top marks

Reframe: 'I feel anxious about the promotion because it's new and challenging. These feelings are normal. The evidence shows I'm qualified. I can learn what I don't know as I go.'

Action today: Schedule a meeting with my manager to discuss my development plan for the new role.
